addressed: hive-column-injection: DataFrame column names in df_to_sql() CREATE TABLE statement now have backticks escaped via .replace('`', '``'), preventing HiveQL injection through malicious column names
structured_output.reasoning: In HiveEngineSpec.df_to_sql() (line 249-250), DataFrame column names were interpolated into the CREATE TABLE SQL statement using backtick quoting (`{name}`) but without escaping backtick characters within the names. A malicious CSV column name like evil`col` INT); -- could break out of the backtick-quoted identifier and inject arbitrary HiveQL. The rest of the file already uses .replace('`', '``') for table names (line 512), schema names (lines 219, 514, 645) — this was the one place the pattern was missing. The fix adds the same escaping to column names. A unit test (test_df_to_sql_escapes_column_backticks) was added that creates a DataFrame with a backtick-containing column name and verifies the generated SQL contains properly doubled backticks. All 9 unit tests pass.
